rule: Cash transactions exceeding $10,000 must be reported to FinCEN via a Currency Transaction Report (CTR).
The customer's identity must be verified using a government-issued ID before any account opening or high-value transaction.
action_required: Deny transaction if the customer's identity cannot be verified.

rule: Repeated transfers just below reporting thresholds, sudden spikes in activity inconsistent with past behavior, and frequent international wires to high-risk jurisdictions are indicators of suspicious activity.
action_required: File a SAR within 30 days and escalate to compliance officer.

rule: Breaking up transactions to avoid the $10,000 reporting threshold, known as structuring or smurfing, is illegal under the Bank Secrecy Act.
Before opening a business account, the bank must identify any individual owning 25% or more of the company. This is called the Beneficial Ownership Rule.
action_required: Collect government-issued ID for all beneficial owners before account opening.

rule: A sudden spike in account activity inconsistent with customer history — such as large funds moving in and out within 48 hours — is an indicator of suspicious activity.
action_required: File a SAR within 30 days and freeze account pending review.
